user_query:
Hey mate, could you do a quick compliance audit of our little film society’s docs so we don’t trip over privacy or public performance rules before our next run of classic British screenings? Please use only the local files under input/ and produce the following outputs:

1) Inspect the directory tree input/ recursively and write an inventory to out/file_inventory.txt listing each discovered file’s relative path and its line count, one file per line.

2) Using the checklist at input/checklists/compliance_checklist.json, extract structured evidence from the documents to determine whether each requirement passes or fails. Treat a requirement as Pass if any of its keywords (case-insensitive) appear in any of the files listed in that requirement’s must_appear_in array; otherwise Fail. For Pass items, collect every matching line in the allowed files as evidence. Save your structured results to out/extracted_hits.json with one object per requirement containing: id, category, description, status ("pass" or "fail"), and evidence (array of objects with file_path, line (1-based), matched_keyword, and line_text). If a requirement fails, evidence should be an empty array.

3) Compose a concise compliance report at out/compliance_report.md that includes: (a) a summary with total requirements, passed, and failed counts; (b) a per-category breakdown with pass/fail counts; and (c) a "Missing requirements" section listing each failed requirement with its id and description, plus a suggested document target derived from the requirement’s must_appear_in (e.g., "Add to: input/policies/privacy_policy.md").

Do not modify any input files or rely on the Internet. The audit should be based strictly on the provided checklist and documents.

Inputs are under input/: policies and contracts to review, plus the checklist JSON. Outputs should be written exactly to out/file_inventory.txt, out/extracted_hits.json, and out/compliance_report.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
